summary: Frontend-only verification of (1) per-region product card currency-code label and (2) region-aware checkout address form (default render, JS country-switch morph, per-region postcode validation). All 8 requested test scenarios pass on the live preview URL. No issues found.

product_card_currency_label.row-currency-<slug>: Not present on default grid view of shop.php (list view not active by default). Not a regression — render_product_row() data-testid is only emitted when the row template is used.

checkout_region_defaults.USD /checkout.php: country=United States, region label='State *', SELECT (AL,AK,AZ,AR,CA,CO,CT,DE,...), postal='ZIP Code *', placeholder='90210', dial=+1, prices in $ ✓
checkout_region_defaults.CAD /ca/checkout.php: country=Canada, region='Province *', SELECT (AB,BC,MB,NB,NL,NS,NT,NU,...), postal='Postal Code *', placeholder='K1A 0B1', dial=+1, prices in CA$ ✓
checkout_region_defaults.AUD /au/checkout.php: country=Australia, region='State/Territory *', SELECT (ACT,NSW,NT,QLD,SA,TAS,VIC,WA), postal='Postcode *', placeholder='2000', dial=+61, prices in AU$ ✓
checkout_region_defaults.GBP /uk/checkout.php: country=United Kingdom, region='County' (no asterisk — not required), INPUT (free-text, tag=INPUT), postal='Postcode *', placeholder='SW1A 1AA', dial=+44, prices in £ ✓
checkout_region_defaults.EUR /eu/checkout.php: country=Europe (Other), region='Region / State' INPUT free-text, postal='Postal Code *', placeholder='10115', dial=+49, prices in € ✓

js_country_switch_morph.window.MV_REGION_FORMS: object with keys [US, CA, UK, AU, EU] ✓
js_country_switch_morph.US->UK: label 'County', tag morphed to INPUT, 'Postcode *' / 'SW1A 1AA', dial +44 ✓
js_country_switch_morph.US->EU: label 'Region / State', INPUT free-text, 'Postal Code *' / '10115', dial +49 ✓

form_submission_au.invalid_postcode_ABCDE: Client validation blocks submit and shows Australian-specific error: 'Australian postcode must be 4 digits (e.g. 2000).' ✓
form_submission_au.valid_postcode_2000_state_NSW: Postcode/state pass validation; remaining errors are only Stripe card fields (Enter your card number / Expiry MM-YY / CVV 3-4 digits) — expected because Stripe demo/test mode requires card entry. Country saved as AU in payload; AU$ shown in summary. ✓

critical_code_review_comments: Checkout JS uses inline onchange='mvApplyCheckoutRegion(this.value)' which works but exposes a global; consider attaching listener via addEventListener inside a DOMContentLoaded handler. (Minor.)

checkout.php is described as ~835+ lines including server config, render and JS — consider extracting MV_REGION_FORMS to includes/region_forms.php for reuse across cart/address books. (Maintainability, non-blocking.)
test_credentials: Not required — guest checkout flow used.
seed_data_creation: Used cart-only fixture: added product slug 'microsoft-office-2024-professional-plus-windows' via /ajax/cart.php?action=add for each region prefix to bypass cart->checkout redirect.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 regional checkout pages (US bare, /ca, /au, /uk, /eu) verified end-to-end via Playwright. Form fields names: email, phone_code, phone, first_name, last_name, address, address2, country (id=co-country, testid=country-select), city, state (testid=state-select; tag SELECT for US/CA/AU, INPUT for UK/EU), zip (id=co-postal, testid=zip-input). Submission uses inline form.requestSubmit() — clicking 'Pay Securely' button is intercepted by a 'testPreviewModal' on first load; use requestSubmit() in scripts. Stripe test/demo mode is by design — card fields will always emit client-side errors unless filled. The currency-label per card uses testid pattern card-currency-<slug>. Row view (row-currency-<slug>) is only rendered when list-mode template is used, not on default grid /shop.php — not a bug.
